items.headline: Auto dealership proposed for El Camino Real commercial site
items.summary: Planning Commission will consider a special development permit to allow auto sales at 975 W. El Camino Real. The proposal involves site improvements like repaving and landscaping but no building expansion or exterior changes.

items.headline: 195-unit apartment complex permitted near Berryessa transit station
items.summary: A $32 million multi-family development at 825 Berryessa Station Way has been cleared to build, adding 195 apartments near the Berryessa BART station. The project supports San José's transit-oriented housing goals in a high-demand area with direct public transportation access.

items.headline: Financial Assistance Pilot Program for Water Customers
items.summary: San José City Council approved a new pilot program to provide financial assistance to water customers. The program aims to help residents afford their water bills, though the specific eligibility criteria and funding amount were not detailed in the agenda.

items.headline: City faces potential lawsuit over Cuesta Park water main incident
items.summary: Mountain View is in closed-session discussions with legal counsel regarding potential litigation stemming from a water main incident at Cuesta Park. The city is also negotiating real property matters related to two adjacent parcels on Clyde Avenue.

items.headline: 27-unit townhome project proposed with 5 affordable units
items.summary: The Planning Commission considered a residential development of 27 three-story townhomes, including 5 affordable units. This mixed-income project represents ongoing housing development in Cupertino.

items.headline: Litigation update: Fantozzi v. City and SummerHill Homes
items.summary: The City Council held a closed session to discuss an ongoing lawsuit (Case No. 26CV492787) involving Mark Fantozzi against the City of Cupertino and SummerHill Homes LLC. An open session report on the closed discussion was scheduled.

items.headline: City Council to hear 72-unit affordable housing project at Lytton Avenue
items.summary: The council will hold a public hearing on a six-story, 100% affordable housing development proposed for 450 Lytton Avenue that would replace a city-owned 52-space parking lot. The project requests density bonus concessions under state law and is seeking expedited streamlined review.

items.headline: Campbell adopts policy restricting use of city property for federal civil enforcement
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution prohibiting city-owned and city-operated property from being used for federal civil enforcement actions. This policy limits cooperation between local facilities and federal agencies in certain enforcement activities.
